phase1.output:
Some generic animal sound
Woof! Woof!
Meow~ Meow~
Moo! Moo!
--- Runtime Polymorphism ---
Woof! Woof!
Meow~ Meow~
phase1.explanation:
Method overriding allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method:

Key rules for overriding:
1. Same method name, parameters, return type
2. Can't be more restrictive with access modifiers
3. Can't throw broader checked exceptions

@Override annotation:
• Optional but recommended
• Tells compiler: "I'm overriding a method"
• Catches errors (if you misspell method name)

Runtime Polymorphism:
• Animal myDog = new Dog(); — Dog object referenced by Animal
• Which method runs is determined at RUNTIME
• The actual object type determines which version runs

Method overriding vs Overloading:
• Override: same signature, different implementation
• Overload: same name, different parameters

⚠️ Can't override static methods — they belong to the class, not object!

phase2.errorMessage:
Dog.java:8: error: method does not override or implement a method from a supertype
    @Override
    ^
1 error

(And if you remove @Override, it compiles but doesn't override! The parent method is still called.)
phase2.explanation:
The @Override annotation caught the error! The method name is 'makesSound' but the parent has 'makeSound'.

Without @Override, this would compile but NOT override the method. The Dog would still use Animal's makeSound()!

This is why @Override is useful — it ensures you're correctly overriding a parent method.

Always use @Override to avoid subtle bugs!

phase3.prompt:
Create a class Shape with method area() that returns 0.0.
Create Rectangle and Circle subclasses that override area():
• Rectangle: area = width * height
• Circle: area = Math.PI * radius * radius

In main, create Shape references to Rectangle and Circle and print their areas.
dialogueHints: Rectangle should have fields width and height, Circle should have radius
Use the @Override annotation on both area() methods
Shape's area() should return 0.0 (or be abstract if you want)
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to override! Give methods a new twist~ 🔄
